name: amplifier-expert
description: THE authoritative consultant for ALL Amplifier ecosystem knowledge. Covers modules, repos, bundles, patterns, and best practices. Consult before implementation.
author: microsoft
llmVerdict: Exceptional breadth and accuracy. Consistently grounded in actual documentation with deep understanding of ecosystem design philosophy. The first agent to consult for any Amplifier work.

quality.docsReason: Clear purpose and install instructions with comprehensive setup wizard examples and usage patterns, but lacks explicit discussion of tradeoffs or when not to use the bundle.
quality.designReason: Proper Amplifier bundle structure with clear install command and rich, well-organized README covering modules, patterns, providers, and usage. Minor gap: context files section not explicitly shown in truncated README excerpt, though comprehensive documentation is present.
quality.codeReason: Prompts are generic and lack thoughtful design—they rely on natural language descriptions without reasoning guidance, error handling patterns, or edge case consideration that would elevate them above basic functionality.

behaviors.name: amplifier-dev-behavior
behaviors.description: Development hygiene and best practices for Amplifier CLI users

behaviors.name: amplifier-expert-behavior
behaviors.description: Authoritative consultant for the complete Amplifier ecosystem

name: foundation-expert
description: THE authoritative expert for Amplifier Foundation applications. Covers bundle composition, agent authoring, patterns, and design decisions. MUST be consulted before writing bundle YAML.
author: microsoft
llmVerdict: Exceptional precision on bundle YAML structure and composition patterns. Significantly reduces authoring errors. Highly recommended as a required checkpoint before any bundle work.

quality.docsReason: README has clear purpose (foundational library for Amplifier), explicit install instructions, multiple usage examples (load/compose/execute, utilities), comprehensive documentation links covering patterns and tradeoffs, and discusses design philosophy including when/how to use the system.
quality.designReason: Exemplary Amplifier bundle: proper structure with comprehensive documentation (BUNDLE_GUIDE.md, AGENT_AUTHORING.md, PATTERNS.md), rich context files covering bundle composition and agent authoring, clear @mention resolution system, utilities for composition, reference content co-located, explicit delegation philosophy, and meticulous prompt design positioning it as authoritative pre-bundle-writing consultant.
quality.codeReason: Well-structured documentation with clear API patterns, practical examples, and comprehensive guides, but lacks specific prompt templates or reasoning guidance for the foundation-expert agent itself.

behaviors.name: behavior-agents
behaviors.description: Agent orchestration capability with enhanced delegate tool. Features: - Two-parameter context inheritance (depth + scope) - Session resume (use full session_id from delegate calls) - Fixed tool inheritance (agent declarations honored) - Multi-agent collaboration patterns Note: Th

behaviors.name: behavior-todo-reminder
behaviors.description: Todo tracking with automatic context reminders and visual progress display

name: superpowers
description: Systematic development methodology with TDD, brainstorming, planning, and execution modes. Enforces quality gates and verification discipline across the full development cycle.
author: microsoft
llmVerdict: Excellent discipline enforcement. The TDD-first approach and verification gates prevent a large class of agent mistakes. The brainstorm-to-execute flow is well-designed and transfers well across project types.

quality.docsReason: README clearly documents purpose (bring Superpowers methodology to Amplifier), audience (Amplifier users), provides two installation methods with examples, explains the complete workflow with modes/agents/skills, discusses when to use the behavior vs. full bundle approach, and acknowledges tradeoffs in composition options.
quality.designReason: Exemplary Amplifier bundle with proper structure (behaviors, agents, modes, context, recipes), rich context files articulating philosophy and instructions, five specialized agents with clear roles, six workflow modes following methodology discipline, composable design pattern, and exceptional care in documentation and prompt architecture.
quality.codeReason: Well-structured bundle with clear methodology, specialized agents, and thoughtful composability patterns, but prompts themselves are not shown in detail and the code organization relies heavily on documentation rather than demonstrating explicit prompt engineering sophistication.

behaviors.name: superpowers-methodology-behavior
behaviors.description: TDD and subagent-driven development methodology with specialized agents, interactive modes, and a skills library.

name: zen-architect
description: Code planning, architecture design, and review agent. Operates in ANALYZE, ARCHITECT, and REVIEW modes. Embodies ruthless simplicity — creates specifications that modular-builder implements.
author: microsoft
llmVerdict: Strong architectural judgment with clear philosophy application. The three-mode design (analyze/architect/review) maps well to real development workflows. Ruthless simplicity principle is consistently enforced.

quality.docsReason: Clear purpose and audience (bundle authors/developers), comprehensive install instructions, multiple usage examples for both library and direct utilities, extensive reference documentation, but lacks discussion of tradeoffs or when NOT to use the foundation bundle.
quality.designReason: This is documentation for a foundational library/framework, not a properly structured Amplifier agent bundle with context files, system prompts, and tool definitions for the zen-architect agent.
quality.codeReason: The bundle provides well-documented utilities and a solid foundation library, but the zen-architect agent itself lacks visible prompt design—no example prompts, reasoning frameworks, or edge-case handling shown for the ANALYZE/ARCHITECT/REVIEW modes it claims to support.

name: modular-builder
description: Implementation-only agent. Requires complete specifications. Executes TDD implementation with pattern references, file paths, and interfaces. Will stop if specification is incomplete.
author: microsoft
llmVerdict: Excellent implementation discipline. The hard requirement for complete specs before starting prevents scope creep. Pairs exceptionally well with zen-architect as a design-then-build workflow.

quality.docsReason: Clear purpose, comprehensive install instructions, and multiple usage examples (quick start, utilities, full workflow) are present; however, lacks explicit discussion of tradeoffs, limitations, or when not to use the bundle.
quality.designReason: Recognizable Amplifier bundle with proper install command and extensive reference documentation, but the bundle itself is a library/foundational package rather than an agent with rich context files, and the modular-builder agent description lacks specific context sinks, tool definitions, or example prompts.
quality.codeReason: Solid documentation with clear API structure, utility organization, and reference patterns, but the agent description itself lacks detailed execution guidance, error-handling specifications, and concrete TDD templates that would elevate it to expert level.

name: bug-hunter
description: Systematic debugging expert. Hypothesis-driven debugging methodology — investigates, identifies root cause, implements targeted fixes. MUST BE USED when errors occur.
author: microsoft
llmVerdict: Exceptional methodological rigor. Consistently identifies root causes rather than patching symptoms. The hypothesis-driven approach prevents the common AI mistake of trying multiple fixes simultaneously.

quality.docsReason: The README is for the foundation library itself, not for the bug-hunter agent bundle—it lacks purpose statement, audience, usage examples, or tradeoffs specific to the bug-hunter agent being documented.
quality.designReason: This is the foundation:foundation bundle itself (a library), not a proper bug-hunter agent bundle—it lacks agent-specific context files, system prompts, debugging methodology documentation, and tool definitions that a bug-hunter agent would require.
quality.codeReason: The bundle describes a debugging agent but provides only generic documentation without concrete debugging prompts, hypothesis-driven reasoning templates, or structured investigation workflows that would constitute clear expertise in systematic debugging methodology.

name: git-ops
description: Safe git and GitHub operations with quality commit messages, PR creation, branch management, and conflict resolution. ALWAYS delegate git operations here for safety protocols.
author: microsoft
llmVerdict: Reliable and safe. The safety protocol guardrails prevent destructive git operations. Commit message quality is notably better than ad-hoc generation. Good GitHub CLI integration for PR workflows.

quality.docsReason: README exists but is for the wrong bundle (foundation library, not git-ops agent) and lacks purpose, installation, usage examples, and tradeoffs specific to the git-ops bundle being evaluated.
quality.designReason: This appears to be documentation for a foundational library rather than a proper git-ops agent bundle—it lacks agent-specific context files, tool definitions, commit message templates, branch management workflows, or safety protocols despite the description claiming them.
quality.codeReason: The 'git-ops' agent description claims to handle safe git operations with quality commits and PR creation, but no actual prompt implementation, code examples, or structured guidance is provided—only a foundation library README unrelated to git operations.

name: explorer
description: Deep local-context reconnaissance agent. Multi-file exploration and codebase survey. Must be used for any exploration task involving more than 2 files — preserves parent context.
author: microsoft
llmVerdict: Solid context-sink pattern. Effectively absorbs token costs of large codebase exploration. The requirement to provide full objective and scope in the instruction is appropriate given the clean-slate design.

quality.docsReason: Clear purpose and audience (bundle authors/Python users), comprehensive install instructions, multiple usage examples (Quick Start, utilities, full workflow), and extensive documentation links, but lacks explicit discussion of tradeoffs or when not to use this foundation bundle versus alternatives.
quality.designReason: This is documentation for a foundational library/framework, not a proper Amplifier agent bundle—it lacks agent-specific context files, tool definitions, system prompts, and the explorer agent description provides no actual implementation or context sink pattern.
quality.codeReason: The README is well-organized reference documentation but contains no actual prompts, agent instructions, or code examples specific to the 'explorer' agent—just library API overview and setup guidance that could apply to any bundle tool.

name: browser-tester
description: Browser automation bundle with operator, researcher, and visual documenter agents. Uses agent-browser CLI for navigation, form filling, data extraction, and screenshot capture.
author: microsoft
llmVerdict: Effective browser automation layer. The three-agent split (operator/researcher/visual) maps well to different automation use cases. Accessibility-tree approach via agent-browser is more robust than DOM selectors.

quality.docsReason: Clear purpose, installation instructions, and multiple usage examples provided; however, lacks discussion of tradeoffs, limitations, or when not to use the bundle.
quality.designReason: Proper Amplifier bundle structure with three well-defined agents, clear installation instructions, helpful recipes and architecture documentation, follows context-sink pattern appropriately, but lacks detailed agent prompt/context files and specific tool/capability documentation in the provided README excerpt.
quality.codeReason: The README provides functional documentation with clear agent descriptions and setup instructions, but lacks substantive prompt design content—no actual prompts are shown, no reasoning guidance for agent behavior is detailed, and the documentation focuses on installation/usage rather than demonstrating prompt quality or edge case handling.

behaviors.name: browser-testing-behavior
behaviors.description: Browser automation behavior - adds browser agents for web interaction, research, and visual documentation. Can be composed into any bundle.

name: python-dev
description: Expert Python development with integrated ruff formatting, pyright type checking, LSP code intelligence, and stub detection. Catches quality issues immediately after edits.
author: microsoft
llmVerdict: Excellent Python tooling integration. The ruff+pyright combination catches a wide class of issues. The LSP code-intel sub-agent provides genuine semantic understanding beyond text search. Well-documented configuration.

quality.docsReason: README has clear purpose (Python dev tools bundle), explicit install instructions, multiple usage examples (YAML config, agent usage, hook behavior), comprehensive architecture documentation, and discusses tradeoffs/limitations (planned features roadmap, development philosophy section explaining when/why to use each component).
quality.designReason: Exemplary Amplifier bundle with proper structure (tool, hook, agent modules), rich context files, clear integration philosophy, comprehensive configuration via pyproject.toml, delegation between CLI/tool/hook to shared core, and detailed development guidance showing obvious care in prompt and architecture design.
quality.codeReason: Well-structured bundle documentation with clear configuration examples and architectural diagrams, but lacks concrete implementation details of actual prompts/code patterns used by the python-dev agent and checker modules.

behaviors.name: python-dev-behavior
behaviors.description: Comprehensive Python development - LSP intelligence + quality tools

behaviors.name: python-quality-behavior
behaviors.description: Python code quality tools - ruff, pyright, stub detection, and expert agent

name: code-navigator
description: Semantic code navigation via Language Server Protocol. Traces call hierarchies, maps dependencies, and understands type flows. Produces semantic results vs grep's text matches.
author: microsoft
llmVerdict: Strong semantic code intelligence. The LSP approach provides genuine type-aware navigation that grep cannot match. The distinction between semantic and text results is clearly maintained in outputs.

quality.docsReason: Clear purpose and audience with comprehensive install instructions and multiple usage examples, but lacks discussion of tradeoffs (e.g., when LSP is insufficient vs grep, performance considerations, or language server prerequisites).
quality.designReason: Proper Amplifier bundle structure with clear agent/tool descriptions, multiple context components (tool-lsp, lsp-core, code-navigator, lsp-general), and thoughtful documentation following delegation philosophy; minor gaps include sparse example prompts and limited context guidance for the code-navigator agent specifically.
quality.codeReason: Documentation explains LSP operations and configuration clearly, but the actual prompt/agent design for the code-navigator is not shown—only bundle structure, installation, and philosophy are documented without revealing the reasoning guidance, edge case handling, or prompt engineering that would indicate expertise in semantic code navigation tasks.

name: recipes
description: Multi-step AI agent orchestration with declarative YAML recipes. Supports sequential execution, context accumulation, approval gates, while loops, and resumability after interruption.
author: microsoft
llmVerdict: Well-designed orchestration layer. The approval gate pattern is particularly valuable for human-in-loop workflows. Resumability after interruption is a genuine differentiator. Schema is clean and learnable.

quality.docsReason: README has clear purpose, explicit install instructions, multiple usage examples (execute recipe, create recipe, session management), comprehensive documentation links, and discusses tradeoffs (auto-cleanup timing, checkpoint frequency, resumability patterns).
quality.designReason: Exemplary Amplifier bundle with proper structure, comprehensive context files (schema, guides, best practices, troubleshooting), clear tool/agent descriptions, delegation philosophy throughout, rich examples and templates, excellent prompt design for agents, and meticulous care in documentation.
quality.codeReason: Solid prompts with clear reasoning guidance (recipe-author agent, result-validator), good schema structure, and contextual examples, but lacks explicit edge-case handling patterns and prompt optimization techniques that would elevate to expert level.

behaviors.name: recipes-behavior
behaviors.description: Multi-step AI agent orchestration via declarative YAML recipes

name: session-analyst
description: REQUIRED for analyzing, debugging, and repairing Amplifier sessions. Performs transcript surgery for orphaned tool calls, ordering violations, and incomplete turns. Handles 100k+ token lines safely.
author: microsoft
llmVerdict: Invaluable for session recovery. The REPAIR-over-REWIND default is the right choice — preserving history is almost always preferable. Safe 100k+ token line handling is a genuine necessity for events.jsonl files.

quality.docsReason: README is for the foundation library, not the session-analyst agent bundle—it lacks purpose statement for session-analyst, no usage examples for the agent's specific functionality (transcript surgery, orphaned tool calls), and provides only a generic install command without explaining what the agent does or when to use it.
quality.designReason: This is the foundation library documentation, not a session-analyst agent bundle; it lacks agent-specific context files, system prompts, tool definitions, and operational guidance for transcript surgery and debugging.
quality.codeReason: README is comprehensive documentation and examples, but the bundle itself lacks substantive prompt design—no reasoning guidance, edge case handling, or clear session-analyst-specific instructions for transcript surgery, orphaned tool calls, or token-safe processing as advertised.

name: security-guardian
description: Security review expert covering OWASP Top 10, hardcoded secrets detection, input/output validation, cryptographic review, and dependency vulnerability scanning. REQUIRED before production.
author: microsoft
llmVerdict: Thorough and systematic security analysis. OWASP Top 10 coverage is comprehensive. The required-before-production gate enforces good practices. Dependency scanning integration catches a class of vulnerabilities often missed.

quality.docsReason: README is for the foundation library itself, not the security-guardian agent bundle; it contains no information about the agent's purpose, security review capabilities, usage examples, or when to use it.
quality.designReason: The provided content is the Foundation library README, not the security-guardian agent bundle itself; no agent-specific prompt, context files, tools description, or security review configuration are present.
quality.codeReason: No actual prompts or code implementation visible—the README describes a Python library/bundle system but contains no security review prompts, detection logic, validation code, or agent instructions needed for the security-guardian agent to function.

name: agent-daemon
description: Local job scheduler running at localhost:7700. Schedule shell commands, Claude AI prompts, and Amplifier recipes on cron, interval, file-watch, or one-shot triggers.
author: kenotron-ms
llmVerdict: Practical local automation solution. The three executor types (shell/claude-code/amplifier) cover the main use cases. macOS tray app is a nice touch. File-watch triggers enable event-driven workflows.

quality.docsReason: README has clear purpose, comprehensive installation instructions (one-liner, manual, from-source), multiple usage examples (quick start, CLI reference, watch trigger config), and discusses tradeoffs (notify vs. poll modes, user vs. system service, environment setup).
quality.designReason: Proper Amplifier bundle structure with clear install command, comprehensive README covering features/installation/CLI, and meaningful integration with Amplifier recipes and Claude Code; however, lacks explicit context files, detailed agent/tool descriptions, and prompt engineering guidance that would elevate it to a 4.
quality.codeReason: Prompts are generic and lack Claude-specific reasoning guidance; the AI assistant feature is mentioned but no example prompts, edge cases, or structured instructions are provided for users to effectively leverage it.

name: core-expert
description: Expert consultant for Amplifier kernel internals. Deep understanding of kernel contracts, module protocols, the event system, hooks API, and session lifecycle. Use for kernel vs module decisions.
author: microsoft
llmVerdict: Authoritative on kernel internals. The litmus test for kernel vs module decisions is consistently applied. Excellent on hooks API and event system. Essential for module authors.

quality.docsReason: Clear purpose (ultra-thin modular AI kernel), install instructions (pip install, development setup with Rust), comprehensive usage examples (session API, module development), and explicit discussion of design philosophy including when mechanisms belong in kernel vs modules.
quality.designReason: Exemplary Amplifier bundle with clear kernel/module philosophy, comprehensive context documentation covering architecture/contracts/APIs, proper install command, rich README explaining mechanisms vs. policies, and obvious expert-level design care for kernel internals consultation.
quality.codeReason: Well-structured documentation with clear architecture diagrams and protocol examples, but prompts lack explicit reasoning guidance for kernel vs. module decisions and edge case handling that the agent description claims to provide.

behaviors.name: core-expert-behavior
behaviors.description: Expert consultant for Amplifier kernel internals

name: amplifier-bundle-agent-memory
description: Semantic memory system for Amplifier agents with vector similarity search. Provides persistent, searchable memory across sessions — find relevant past context without reading everything.
author: ramparte
llmVerdict: Well-executed semantic memory layer. Vector similarity search is more effective than naive context injection. The cross-session persistence design is solid. High value for long-running projects where context drift is a real problem.

quality.docsReason: README has clear purpose (semantic memory for agents), explicit installation instructions, concrete usage examples (storing/searching memories), detailed configuration, architecture diagram, tool specifications, AND discusses tradeoffs (costs, limits, performance, security considerations).
quality.designReason: Exemplary bundle with proper Amplifier structure, two well-designed tools (memory_store/memory_search), comprehensive configuration documentation, clear security considerations, performance metrics, cost analysis, and thoughtful agent isolation via namespacing.
quality.codeReason: README documents features and configuration clearly but provides no prompts, code examples for actual agent integration, or guidance on designing effective memory queries—making it functional documentation rather than a quality code/prompt bundle.

name: amplifier-bundle-self-driving
description: Self-driving orchestrator — autonomous task decomposition, adversarial review, crash recovery, and context-aware self-management. Keeps agents on track through long autonomous runs.
author: ramparte
llmVerdict: Innovative self-management approach. Adversarial review built into the loop catches a class of errors that single-agent runs miss. Crash recovery is practical and well-implemented. Pairs well with dev-machine for very long autonomous runs.

quality.docsReason: README is essentially empty with only a title and no purpose explanation, installation instructions, usage examples, audience definition, or any useful documentation about the bundle's functionality.
quality.designReason: Bundle declares proper structure and install command, but README is essentially empty (truncated to 8000 chars shows only header), providing no context files, agent descriptions, tool definitions, or prompt guidance.
quality.codeReason: README is empty (truncated to only triple backticks), making it impossible to evaluate prompt quality, code structure, reasoning guidance, or edge case handling.

behaviors.name: self-driving-behavior
behaviors.description: Self-driving orchestration capability - task decomposition, adversarial review, state tracking, and self-managing execution loop.

name: amplifier-bundle-workgraph
description: Dependency-aware task graph coordination for Amplifier. Define work items with explicit dependencies, let the orchestrator schedule parallel-safe execution across agents.
author: ramparte
llmVerdict: Solid dependency graph approach to task orchestration. Parallel-safe scheduling is the right abstraction for complex multi-agent workflows. The declarative dependency model is clear and prevents ordering bugs.

quality.docsReason: Clear purpose and bidirectional integration story, install instructions provided, and multiple usage examples shown; however, lacks explicit discussion of tradeoffs, limitations, or when *not* to use workgraph decomposition.
quality.designReason: Exemplary bundle structure with dual integration patterns, comprehensive context files (workgraph-guide, executor protocol), specialized agent, clear installation/configuration, well-documented architecture, and thoughtful executor design that demonstrates deep understanding of both systems.
quality.codeReason: Solid integration documentation with clear architectural guidance and practical examples, but prompts lack explicit reasoning chains, edge-case handling is minimal, and the behavior/executor configs lack validation or error-handling patterns.

behaviors.name: workgraph-behavior
behaviors.description: Adds workgraph task graph awareness and tooling to Amplifier agents

name: amplifier-bundle-metacognition
description: Metacognitive agents inspired by Poetiq's ARC-AGI approach. Implements self-monitoring, strategy selection, and performance reflection loops for more reliable autonomous behavior.
author: ramparte
llmVerdict: Genuinely experimental but well-grounded. The ARC-AGI inspiration translates into concrete self-monitoring behaviors. Strategy selection layer adds measurable improvement on complex tasks. Worth watching as the approach matures.

quality.docsReason: Excellent documentation with clear purpose, detailed installation instructions, multiple concrete usage examples across different complexity scenarios, comprehensive agent descriptions, and thoughtful discussion of when to use each component.
quality.designReason: Exemplary Amplifier bundle with proper structure, rich context files (poetiq-patterns.md, complexity-signals.md, scoring-rubrics.md), four well-defined specialized agents with clear input/output schemas, comprehensive delegation philosophy, and meticulous prompt design demonstrating deep care in orchestration.
quality.codeReason: Well-structured prompts with clear agent personas and reasoning guidance (complexity assessment, iterative refinement, soft scoring), but lacks explicit edge case handling and relies on implicit patterns rather than explicit prompt engineering best practices.

name: amplifier-module-tool-diagrams
description: Diagram generation tool for Amplifier via Mermaid, D2, and other formats through Kroki. Generate architecture diagrams, flowcharts, sequence diagrams, and more from code or prose.
author: ramparte
llmVerdict: Practical diagram generation with good format coverage. Mermaid + D2 + Kroki covers the main use cases. Useful for documentation workflows and architecture visualization. The Kroki backend handles format diversity cleanly.

quality.docsReason: Clear purpose and audience, good installation instructions with multiple usage examples covering generation/validation/rendering, but lacks discussion of tradeoffs or limitations (e.g., Kroki.io dependency, when to use alternatives).
quality.designReason: Proper Amplifier bundle structure with clear tool description, meaningful context files (installation, quick start, supported types, documentation references), and well-defined operations (generate, validate, render, templates), though context files are referenced rather than embedded and prompt design guidance could be more explicit.
quality.codeReason: Prompts are functional but generic—the examples show basic operation calls without reasoning guidance, edge-case handling, or structured patterns that distinguish this tool's capabilities from standard diagram libraries.

name: agent-governance-toolkit
description: AI Agent Governance Toolkit — policy enforcement, zero-trust identity, execution sandboxing, and reliability engineering for autonomous agents. Covers all 10 OWASP Agentic Top 10 risks.
author: marklicata
llmVerdict: Comprehensive governance coverage. OWASP Agentic Top 10 alignment is timely and thorough. Zero-trust identity model is the right approach for agent-to-agent interactions. High value for enterprise or production deployments.

quality.docsReason: Exceptional documentation with clear purpose (runtime governance for AI agents), well-defined audience (developers building governed agents), comprehensive installation instructions for Python/TypeScript/.NET, multiple usage examples (policy enforcement in all languages), detailed quick start, and extensive discussion of tradeoffs (community preview status, vendor independence, performance benchmarks, OWASP coverage mapping).
quality.designReason: Exemplary Amplifier bundle with comprehensive structure: multi-language SDKs (Python/TypeScript/.NET), rich documentation spanning quickstarts, tutorials, architecture, OWASP compliance mapping, clear installation paths, practical code examples across all languages, and thoughtful agent governance context that demonstrates deep domain expertise and user-centric design.
quality.codeReason: Solid prompts with clear governance patterns (policy enforcement, zero-trust identity, sandboxing) and multi-language examples, but lacks advanced reasoning guidance, edge-case handling, and explicit prompt engineering best practices for agent-specific scenarios.

name: amplifier-bundle-bundlewizard
description: Guided scaffold for designing, creating, and validating new Amplifier bundles. Interviews you on intent, generates bundle structure, validates schema, and writes the YAML and context files.
author: michaeljabbour
llmVerdict: Excellent bundle authoring tool. The interview-then-generate approach produces better starting points than blank templates. Schema validation integration catches errors early. Complements foundation-expert well as a practical authoring companion.

quality.docsReason: Comprehensive README with clear purpose, detailed install instructions, multiple usage examples (create, improve, upgrade, autonomous modes), full explanation of how it works, architectural diagrams, and explicit discussion of tradeoffs (interactive vs. autonomous modes, quality gates).
quality.designReason: Exemplary Amplifier bundle with clear dual-track architecture (interactive/autonomous), comprehensive agent and recipe design, detailed mode descriptions, quality gates via three-level convergence system, and sophisticated context-sink patterns through adversarial critique loops.
quality.codeReason: Well-structured prompts with clear reasoning guidance (adversarial critic/evaluator, convergence loops, three-level verification), good edge case handling (upgrade detection, autonomous vs. interactive modes), but lacks explicit prompt engineering patterns and could show more depth in how individual agents handle ambiguity or failure modes.

behaviors.name: bundlewizard-behavior
behaviors.description: Core behavior for bundlewizard. Mounts the modes system, registers all agents, loads skills, and includes root-level context.

name: amplifier-bundle-loop-safety
description: Prevents runaway Amplifier agent loops via iteration limits and pattern detection. Detects repetitive tool calls, circular reasoning, and stalled progress — surfaces them for human intervention.
author: michaeljabbour
llmVerdict: Addresses a real failure mode. Pattern detection for repetitive tool calls is more sophisticated than simple iteration limits. The human-intervention surface is well-designed. Essential for any long-running autonomous workflow.

quality.docsReason: Documentation excels with clear purpose, detailed install instructions, comprehensive usage examples (Options A & B, configuration profiles), configuration reference tables, diagnostic capabilities, and explicit discussion of tradeoffs (different safety levels for research vs. production vs. monitoring scenarios).
quality.designReason: Exemplary bundle with proper modular structure (orchestrator + hook components), rich configuration profiles (conservative/development/monitoring), clear delegation philosophy, comprehensive documentation, real-world problem motivation, event emission design, and obvious care in safety mechanism composition.
quality.codeReason: Solid bundle design with clear dual-mechanism approach (orchestrator limits + pattern detection), comprehensive configuration profiles, and good documentation; minor weaknesses include limited code examples for hook implementation details and no explicit edge case handling documentation.

behaviors.name: loop-safety-behavior
behaviors.description: Loop safety behavior providing iteration limits and pattern detection. Includes: - orchestrator-loop-safe: Hard iteration limits with warnings - hooks-loop-detector: Repetitive pattern detection - loop-diagnostician: Diagnostic agent for stuck sessions

name: amplifier-bundle-sage
description: Strategic advisor bundle for Amplifier — enables long-running autonomous work with Gemini-powered strategic counsel. Provides high-level guidance and course correction for complex multi-session projects.
author: michaeljabbour
llmVerdict: Interesting architectural pattern — separating strategic counsel from tactical execution. Gemini integration for high-level guidance is well-motivated. Most effective on projects spanning multiple sessions where drift is a genuine risk.

quality.docsReason: Excellent documentation with clear purpose (strategic advisor bundle), explicit audience (Amplifier users doing autonomous work), comprehensive installation instructions, 12 detailed usage examples across different patterns, and extensive discussion of tradeoffs, constraints, and when/how to use the tool effectively.
quality.designReason: Exemplary bundle with proper Amplifier structure, comprehensive context files covering Core Formula philosophy, 12 detailed autonomous work patterns, clear tool/agent descriptions with usage examples, environment setup, and thoughtful prompt design emphasizing outcomes and constraints.
quality.codeReason: Solid prompts with clear structure and outcome-focused guidance (Core Formula pattern), though lacking edge-case handling and could provide more explicit reasoning about when each pattern applies.

name: amplifier-bundle-cicd
description: Designs, generates, audits, and maintains CI/CD pipelines for applications. Understands GitHub Actions, Azure Pipelines, and other systems — generates pipeline YAML, identifies gaps, and suggests improvements.
author: michaeljabbour
llmVerdict: Strong CI/CD coverage. The audit capability is particularly valuable — identifying gaps in existing pipelines is harder than generating new ones. Multi-system support is practical for mixed environments.

quality.docsReason: Clear purpose, install instructions, and quick-start examples provided; however, lacks discussion of tradeoffs, limitations, or when not to use the bundle.
quality.designReason: Excellent Amplifier bundle with proper modular structure, rich context files (instructions, philosophy, schemas, stage primitives, platform guides), clear architectural abstraction (APR pattern), multiple well-defined entry points, and obvious investment in prompt design and delegation philosophy.
quality.codeReason: Solid architecture with clear separation of concerns (APR abstraction, platform-agnostic design) and well-documented entry points, but prompts themselves are not shown in detail and the implementation relies heavily on downstream agent delegation without visible reasoning guidance or edge-case handling in the bundle's core routing logic.

name: amplifier-bundle-parallax-discovery
description: Multi-agent, multi-pass deep investigation methodology. Deploys multiple independent agents to investigate the same system from different angles, then synthesizes findings for true understanding of complex codebases.
author: bkrabach
llmVerdict: Outstanding methodology for deep codebase understanding. The multi-angle approach catches blind spots that single-agent exploration consistently misses. Synthesis quality is high. Particularly valuable for onboarding to unfamiliar large codebases.

quality.docsReason: README clearly establishes purpose (multi-agent investigation methodology), audience (developers investigating complex systems), install instructions, extensive usage examples for all four waves and recipes, AND explicitly discusses tradeoffs with 'When to Use' and 'Don't use it when' sections plus cost-benefit analysis.
quality.designReason: Exemplary Amplifier bundle with comprehensive structure (behaviors, agents, modes, context, skills, recipes), rich context files articulating methodology and evidence standards, clear agent personas with distinct roles, sophisticated delegation philosophy (multi-pass waves with approval gates), and meticulous prompt design throughout.
quality.codeReason: Exceptionally well-structured bundle with clear separation of concerns (agents, modes, waves), explicit methodology documentation, sophisticated multi-pass investigation patterns with approval gates, edge case handling (when to use/not use), and evidence-based reconciliation logic that reflects deep design thought.

behaviors.name: parallax-discovery-behavior
behaviors.description: Multi-agent deep investigation methodology with specialized agents, interactive modes, and a methodology skills library.

name: amplifier-bundle-unified-llm
description: Rust-native unified LLM provider bundle for Amplifier — supports Anthropic, OpenAI, and Gemini via gRPC sidecar pattern. Zero Python overhead. Significant latency improvement over Python provider stack.
author: bkrabach
llmVerdict: Impressive engineering. The Rust + gRPC sidecar pattern cleanly separates provider logic from Python overhead. Multi-provider support in a single binary is operationally clean. Latency improvements are measurable. Reference implementation quality.

quality.docsReason: Clear purpose, installation instructions, usage examples with available variants, and architecture explanation; however, lacks discussion of tradeoffs, limitations, or when not to use this bundle.
quality.designReason: Proper Amplifier bundle structure with clear provider variants, well-documented gRPC sidecar architecture, and meaningful context organization, though specific context file contents and detailed agent/tool guidance are not shown in the truncated README.
quality.codeReason: The bundle provides functional architecture documentation and variant configuration examples, but lacks depth in prompt engineering, concrete usage patterns, edge case handling, and practical guidance for developers integrating the unified LLM provider.

name: amplifier-hive-slack
description: Multi-agent collaboration via Slack for Amplifier. Route tasks to specialized agents through Slack channels, coordinate long-running work across agents and humans in shared workspaces.
author: bkrabach
llmVerdict: Novel human-agent collaboration pattern via Slack. Channel-based routing to specialized agents is intuitive for teams. Coordination across long-running work is well-handled. Slack as a coordination surface reduces the need for custom UIs.

quality.docsReason: README is exceptionally clear with explicit purpose, target audience, comprehensive install instructions with setup wizard, multiple usage examples in both dialogue and code formats, detailed configuration options, architecture explanation, and discusses tradeoffs (e.g., Slack free tier limitations, when to use DMs vs threads vs channels).
quality.designReason: Proper Amplifier bundle with clear structure, comprehensive README with setup instructions, multiple instance support, and thoughtful context (file sharing, service management, architecture diagram), though lacks explicit context files in the bundle itself and could provide more detailed agent/tool prompting guidance.
quality.codeReason: Solid prompts and well-structured interaction patterns (thread semantics, mid-execution steering, channel routing) with clear reasoning, but lacks edge-case guidance for prompt fragility (e.g., handling ambiguous instance names, recovering from malformed channel topics) and relies on implicit user mental models rather than explicit prompt engineering principles.

name: amplifier-bundle-dotnet-ops
description: .NET CLI specialist agent for Amplifier — cross-platform .NET development operations including build, test, package management, project scaffolding, and NuGet integration.
author: colombod
llmVerdict: Comprehensive .NET coverage. Build, test, package, and scaffold operations all present. Cross-platform behavior is correct. NuGet integration is clean. Fills a real gap — .NET is underserved in the Amplifier ecosystem relative to Python.

quality.docsReason: README clearly defines purpose (cross-platform .NET CLI agent), provides multiple installation methods, includes comprehensive usage examples across 12+ operations with example prompts, explains how the system works with a flowchart, documents platform differences, discusses safety constraints/tradeoffs, and covers bundle structure and prerequisites.
quality.designReason: Exemplary bundle with proper Amplifier structure (bundle.md, agents/, behaviors/, context/), rich context files including delegation instructions, comprehensive agent description with cross-platform awareness, clear tool taxonomy, safety protocols, and meticulous prompt design for platform detection and shell selection.
quality.codeReason: Solid prompts with clear cross-platform reasoning and safety guards, but lacks explicit example prompts in agent definitions and edge-case handling documentation for complex scenarios.

behaviors.name: dotnet-ops-behavior
behaviors.description: Adds dotnet-ops agent with cross-platform shell support. Compose onto any bundle for .NET CLI capabilities on Windows, Linux, or macOS.

name: amplifier-bundle-perplexity
description: Deep research capabilities via Perplexity's Agentic Research API. Enables AI-powered web research with source attribution, multi-source synthesis, and citation tracking inside Amplifier sessions.
author: colombod
llmVerdict: Strong research augmentation. Perplexity Agentic API provides better web research than raw browsing for many use cases. Source attribution is reliable. Good complement to the specialists bundle for research-heavy workflows.

quality.docsReason: README clearly explains purpose (deep web research via Perplexity API), provides three installation options with commands, includes multiple practical usage examples (direct tool use, agent delegation, chaining), discusses cost tradeoffs and when to use alternatives, and offers comprehensive feature documentation with recipes and API reference.
quality.designReason: Exemplary bundle with proper Amplifier structure, rich context files detailing tool/agent capabilities, domain-specific query enhancement, categorized references with URLs for downstream agents, clear delegation philosophy, cost-awareness guidance, and meticulous prompt design throughout.
quality.codeReason: Solid prompts with clear domain-aware query enhancement and structured agent delegation patterns, but lacks edge-case handling guidance and reusable prompt templates for common research scenarios.

behaviors.name: perplexity-research-behavior
behaviors.description: Perplexity deep research capability - agent, tool, and guidance

name: amplifier-module-tool-nano-banana
description: Gemini VLM tool for visual analysis, comparison, and image generation. Supports analyze, compare, and generate operations. Used across the ecosystem for mockup generation and visual convergence loops.
author: kenotron-ms
llmVerdict: Strong VLM integration with practical three-operation design (analyze/compare/generate). Reference image support prevents fidelity loss through the text bottleneck. Used heavily in the UI Studio pipeline.

quality.docsReason: README provides clear purpose (VLM tool for mockup-to-code workflows), multiple installation methods with examples, comprehensive operation documentation with input/output examples, use cases, and discusses tradeoffs (why this tool is independent/focused/simple).
quality.designReason: Excellent Amplifier bundle with proper structure (bundle.md, behaviors/, agents/, context/), rich documentation covering all three operations, clear delegation pattern via nano-banana-expert agent, multiple installation paths, and meticulous prompt design with concrete examples.
quality.codeReason: Solid prompts with clear operational examples and structured guidance for analyze/compare/generate, but lacks edge case handling, error recovery patterns, and reasoning depth beyond template demonstration.

behaviors.description: Adds Gemini VLM capabilities — image analysis, comparison, and generation

name: amplifier-voice
description: Voice assistant combining OpenAI Realtime API with Amplifier for tool execution. Speak to your agent, have it execute tools and agents, hear the response. Low-latency voice loop with full Amplifier tool access.
author: bkrabach
llmVerdict: Clean voice integration. OpenAI Realtime API + Amplifier tool access is a compelling combination. Latency is acceptable for conversational use. Good interruption handling.

quality.docsReason: No README file exists for this bundle, making it impossible to assess documentation quality, usage examples, or audience clarity.
quality.designReason: Minimal bundle structure with install command present but no README, context files, or meaningful agent/tool documentation to guide usage or understand the voice assistant implementation.
quality.codeReason: No README or documentation provided, making it impossible to evaluate prompt quality, code structure, or implementation details beyond the basic bundle metadata.

name: amplifier-bundle-attention-firewall
description: AI-powered Windows notification controller — filters notifications, surfaces what matters, batches the rest. Uses Amplifier to classify and triage system notifications in real time.
author: bkrabach
llmVerdict: Creative ambient-computing use of Amplifier. Real-time notification classification is effective. Batching non-urgent notifications reduces interruption meaningfully.

quality.docsReason: README clearly defines purpose (AI-powered notification filtering), target audience (Windows users), provides comprehensive install instructions including prerequisites and config setup, multiple usage examples (Quick Start, CLI commands, configuration), and discusses tradeoffs (client vs standalone mode, noise suppression benefits, setup complexity for auto-start).
quality.designReason: Proper Amplifier bundle structure with clear install command, comprehensive README documenting features/setup/CLI, meaningful context (notification filtering policies, VIP management, digest scheduling), and well-designed agent philosophy (context-sink for notification triage); minor gaps in explicit tool/agent description within the bundle structure itself and limited visibility into actual prompt design or context file organization.
quality.codeReason: The bundle provides functional but generic installation and CLI documentation with shallow prompt design—no reasoning guidance for AI classification logic, no edge case handling in the filtering pipeline, and the configuration examples lack structured prompts that would guide the AI model to make nuanced notification triage decisions.

name: amplifier-bundle-notify
description: Desktop and terminal notifications for Amplifier — fires when the assistant turn completes. Lets you run long tasks and come back when done. Supports macOS, Linux, and Windows.
author: colombod
llmVerdict: Simple and high-value. Solves the "is it done yet?" workflow problem elegantly. Cross-platform support is correct. One of the more universally useful QoL bundles.

quality.docsReason: README clearly explains purpose (notifications for long-running tasks), target audience (Amplifier users), provides multiple installation methods with examples (CLI and manual config), detailed usage instructions, security considerations, platform requirements, and discusses tradeoffs (policy behaviors, ntfy privacy concerns, when to use each notification method).
quality.designReason: Exemplary bundle with proper structure, comprehensive context (security guidance, platform requirements, configuration options), clear policy-behavior philosophy, CLI tooling, multiple notification methods, and thoughtful extensibility via hooks.
quality.codeReason: Well-structured documentation with clear setup paths (CLI, manual config, direct inclusion), security warnings for ntfy topics, and extensibility via hooks, but lacks code examples for the actual prompt/implementation and edge-case handling details.

behaviors.name: behavior-desktop-notifications
behaviors.description: Desktop and terminal notifications when assistant turns complete. Supports macOS, Linux, Windows/WSL, and SSH sessions.

behaviors.name: behavior-push-notifications
behaviors.description: Push notifications via ntfy.sh when assistant turns complete. Perfect for mobile SSH clients (Termius, JuiceSSH) where terminal escape sequences don't trigger system notifications.

name: amplifier-bundle-foreman
description: Foreman orchestration pattern — a foreman session interacts with the user and manages a pool of worker sessions that do the actual work. Clean separation of planning and execution.
author: payneio
llmVerdict: Clean architectural pattern. The foreman-worker split prevents a common failure mode where one session tries to both plan and execute. Workers are disposable; foreman stays coherent.

quality.docsReason: Clear purpose, installation instructions, and multiple usage examples covering basic usage, configuration, and worker bundles, though it lacks explicit discussion of tradeoffs or when not to use the pattern.
quality.designReason: Exemplary Amplifier bundle with clear foreman-worker orchestration pattern, comprehensive configuration examples, well-documented architecture, specialized worker bundle guidance, multiple testing strategies, and thoughtful UX patterns demonstrating deep understanding of delegation philosophy.
quality.codeReason: Solid documentation with well-structured configuration examples and clear architectural diagrams, but prompts and code guidance lack detailed reasoning patterns, edge-case handling, and specific LLM instruction design.

name: amplifier-bundle-session-guardian
description: Automatic session scope management — tracks token pressure, persists state to files, and enables clean handoff between sessions. Prevents context overflow degradation on long-running work.
author: cpark4x
llmVerdict: Addresses a real and frequent pain point. Token pressure tracking is practical. State persistence to files is the right approach. Clean session boundaries are a significant QoL improvement.

quality.docsReason: Clear purpose and install instructions with usage examples (configuration, behavior table), but lacks explicit discussion of tradeoffs, limitations, or when not to use this bundle.
quality.designReason: Exemplary bundle design with clear Amplifier structure, concrete hook/tool architecture, smart context-sink philosophy (token pressure tracking), detailed behavioral thresholds, configuration guidance, and obvious care in solving a real long-running session problem.
quality.codeReason: Solid design with clear thresholds and state management patterns, but prompts lack explicit reasoning guidance for edge cases (e.g., what constitutes 'concise mode', how to handle partial saves, recovery from corrupted state files).

name: amplifier-bundle-discovery
description: Progressive discovery and user interviewing system for Amplifier. Conducts structured interviews to surface requirements, constraints, and preferences before acting.
author: anderlpz
llmVerdict: Thoughtful product discovery approach. Progressive interviewing prevents acting on shallow requirements. The structured interview format extracts constraints users often don't volunteer upfront.

quality.docsReason: Clear purpose and audience, comprehensive installation instructions, multiple usage examples (standalone, recipe-based, embedded), and detailed feature explanations present; however, lacks discussion of tradeoffs, limitations, or when NOT to use the bundle.
quality.designReason: Proper Amplifier bundle structure with clear agent definition, rich methodology context files, and thoughtful prompt design philosophy; minor gaps in actual context file content depth and concrete tool specifications.
quality.codeReason: Well-structured bundle with clear methodology and composable design, but prompts lack explicit reasoning guidance, edge case handling, and the core agent implementation details are truncated, limiting assessment of actual prompt quality.

name: engram
description: Agentic memory augmentation for AI agents — external memory with automatic capture, intelligent retrieval, and cognitive compute offload. Compatible with Amplifier and Claude Code.
author: kenotron-ms
llmVerdict: Elegant memory augmentation design. Automatic capture is better than requiring explicit "remember this". Intelligent retrieval surfaces relevant past context without overwhelming the current session.

quality.docsReason: Excellent documentation with clear purpose, comprehensive install instructions, detailed usage examples (memory tools, dual-route retrieval, space separation), configuration options, and explicit discussion of when/how to use different routes and memory spaces.
quality.designReason: Exemplary Amplifier bundle with comprehensive dual-database architecture, eight well-designed memory tools with clear semantics, sophisticated dual-route retrieval (System-1/System-2), rich configuration options, privacy-aware space separation, and meticulous prompt engineering for silent hook injection—demonstrates exceptional care in cognitive compute offload design.
quality.codeReason: Solid prompt design with clear tool specifications, dual-route retrieval logic, and privacy gates, but lacks explicit edge-case handling and reasoning guidance for when/why to use each memory tool.

name: amplifier-module-tool-stt-whispercpp
description: Local speech-to-text tool for Amplifier using whisper.cpp — fully offline, no API keys required. High accuracy transcription running locally on CPU or GPU for privacy-sensitive workflows.
author: sadlilas
llmVerdict: Valuable for privacy-sensitive environments. whisper.cpp delivers strong accuracy without cloud round-trips. No API key requirement lowers the barrier for enterprise use.

quality.docsReason: Clear purpose, installation instructions, multiple usage examples (mount plan, programmatic, model management), and model selection table present; lacks discussion of tradeoffs (e.g., when to use smaller vs. larger models, performance implications, or limitations).
quality.designReason: Proper Amplifier tool bundle with clear structure, meaningful README covering features/usage/models, appropriate install command, and good context for agent integration; minor gap is absence of explicit context files or delegation examples in documentation.
quality.codeReason: Documentation is clear and functional but prompts lack reasoning guidance, edge case handling, or structured decision-making patterns for LLM interactions—it reads as a library reference rather than a tool designed for intelligent agent prompting.

name: amplifier-module-provider-github-copilot
description: Enables access to GitHub Copilot models via the Copilot SDK inside Amplifier. Use Copilot's model pool — GPT-4o, o1, o3 — through the Amplifier provider interface without separate API keys.
author: samueljklee
llmVerdict: High-value provider for enterprise users. Copilot entitlements cover Amplifier usage without separate billing. Unlocks Amplifier for organizations already paying for Copilot.

quality.docsReason: Excellent documentation with clear purpose, comprehensive installation instructions (multiple OS examples), multiple usage examples (interactive, one-shot, model listing), detailed authentication options, supported models list, troubleshooting table, development setup, and realistic discussion of when/how to use the provider with feature callouts.
quality.designReason: Proper Amplifier provider module with clear installation instructions, comprehensive authentication setup, well-documented models and features, and sensible defaults; minor gaps in explicit context files and agent-specific guidance reduce it from a 4.
quality.codeReason: Well-structured README with clear prerequisites, multiple authentication paths, platform-specific instructions, and practical troubleshooting; however, prompts/code examples are minimal and lack advanced reasoning guidance or edge-case handling patterns.

name: amplifier-bundle-lsp-typescript
description: TypeScript/JavaScript LSP bundle for Amplifier — semantic code intelligence via typescript-language-server. Find references, go-to-definition, hover types, and rename across large TS/JS codebases.
author: robotdad
llmVerdict: Excellent TypeScript intelligence. typescript-language-server provides accurate semantic results on large codebases. Complements python-dev for mixed-language projects.

quality.docsReason: README clearly explains purpose and target audience, provides comprehensive install instructions, multiple usage examples with actual queries, discusses supported project types and when to use (all JS ecosystem projects), includes configuration options, troubleshooting, and philosophy section.
quality.designReason: Proper Amplifier bundle structure with clear LSP configuration, meaningful context files (behaviors, agents, context), well-documented README with examples and troubleshooting, though agent specialization and context depth could be richer.
quality.codeReason: Prompts are functional but generic—they read as documentation examples rather than carefully engineered prompts with reasoning guidance, edge-case handling, or structured chains that distinguish this bundle from a generic LSP tool.
